schema_notes: Per-billionaire v3 record, schema matches larry_ellison.v3.json. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Druckenmiller is the textbook counter-example to Ellison: did NOT sign the Giving Pledge (explicitly declined), yet has given multiples of what he is expected to have given under the 10% norm. Tier A VERIFIED ON TRACK / FULFILLING.

person.wealth_source: Duquesne Capital (founded 1981, closed 2010 with $12B AUM); former lead portfolio manager at Soros Fund Management (1988-2000, including the 1992 Black Wednesday sterling trade); now runs Duquesne Family Office (~$10.8B AUM per Bloomberg Dec 2024)

net_worth.range_note: Range reflects methodology divergence. Bloomberg Billionaires Index / Glen Bradford 2026 estimate ~$10-12B; Forbes 2026 profile ~$2.8B (Forbes methodology likely treats Duquesne Family Office AUM as client assets, not personal); Grok citing 2025 data ~$6.9B; Grizzly Bulls ~$8.1B. Family office reported $10.8B AUM (Dec 2024, Bloomberg), but AUM != net worth. Best estimate settled at $10B midpoint — the most-frequently-cited figure across tracker services.

sources.caveat: Forbes rank #477 and $2.8B figure is much lower than Bloomberg — Forbes likely excludes Duquesne Family Office AUM that is partly Druckenmiller's own capital.

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Wealth is overwhelmingly liquid. Duquesne Family Office holdings are marketable securities disclosed quarterly on 13F. No operating company stake, no concentrated founder position. Apply 85% liquidity (highest tier — only MacKenzie Scott and cash-position hedge managers are comparable). This massively raises the 'expected giving' denominator relative to similarly-valued billionaires with concentrated stock holdings.

cited_events.extraction_note: Stanley Druckenmiller made a contribution to the pro-Scott Opportunity Matters Fund Action PAC in the first half of 2023, as part of a group of three billionaires whose combined contributions totaled almost $1 million.

cited_events.quote: I don't really see the purpose of my signing the Giving Pledge. People worth hundreds of billions of dollars saying they're going to give half of their worth away — I don't think that really moves the needle on drawing in other philanthropists. Maybe because of my Pittsburgh background, I enjoy Andrew Carnegie's approach of being as anonymous as possible.
cited_events.notes: CRITICAL CORRECTION: Druckenmiller did NOT sign the Giving Pledge in 2010 or any year. He has publicly stated he declined. He is NOT on the official Giving Pledge list at givingpledge.org. His critique: that the Pledge is performative for ultra-high-net-worth donors who will never disburse. This makes him a rare case of a 'verified on-track' giver who is NOT a pledger — evidence that pledge-signing and actual giving are orthogonal. Under IPS methodology he cannot be classified as 'pledge unfulfilled' because there is no pledge.

pledges_and_announcements.notes: Druckenmiller closed Duquesne Capital Management in August 2010 (then $12B AUM) explicitly citing desire to spend more time on philanthropy. Reconstituted as Duquesne Family Office. This is a rare case of a hedge fund manager voluntarily stepping back from the business to focus on giving.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1. However, Druckenmiller's political totals are small relative to his foundation giving (roughly 1:100 political:charity ratio), so substitution concern is minimal.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Rough floor from press sources. Includes ~$300K in 2015 (Christie/Bush/Kasich); $152K in 2018 cycle; $150K to Tim Scott super-PAC (Opportunity Matters Fund Action, April 2023); ongoing Nikki Haley support in 2024 primary. Druckenmiller publicly stated he voted for neither Trump nor Harris in Nov 2024. Pattern is fiscal-conservative/establishment-Republican primary donor — NOT a Trump-aligned mega-donor. Dollar figure will be tightened when Phase 1.3 FEC bulk pull runs against his CRP ID U0000004266.

foundations_active.name: Druckenmiller Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (all three officers — Stanley Druckenmiller trustee, Fiona Druckenmiller trustee, Gerald Kerner admin officer — received $0 across all years)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants out ($135.8M) / assets ($1.77B) = 7.7% — above the 5% legal minimum. Average 2019-2024 annual grants ~$110M/yr. No LOW_PAYOUT flag.
foundations_active.overhead_notes: Inside Philanthropy reports ~2.2% overhead — exceptionally lean. Foundation has no website, no public-facing staff, no application process. Operates as a concentrated 'big bet' grantmaker: average grant size $2.1M, ~97% of dollars go to $1M+ grants.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED_BUT_UNLIKELY (Blue Meridian Partners is the single largest grant recipient — Blue Meridian is a collaborative fund, NOT a DAF, so the high concentration to one recipient is not a DAF red flag)

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No donor-advised fund activity identified in public reporting. Druckenmiller's 'Carnegie-style anonymity' preference makes unreported DAF activity plausible, but absence-of-evidence only. Apply standard cohort uplift.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC. Duquesne Family Office is the personal investment vehicle, NOT a philanthropic LLC like CZI or Emerson Collective.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2.

collaborative_funds_chaired.name: Blue Meridian Partners
collaborative_funds_chaired.classification_note: Blue Meridian is a 501(c)(3) collaborative fund, not a DAF. Contributions from Druckenmiller Foundation TO Blue Meridian are grants-out from the donor side and transfers-in from Blue Meridian's side. For Druckenmiller's rollup: count as grant_out (already inside foundation grants_out totals). Blue Meridian is one of the most academically-respected funder collaboratives in the US — pools capital for performance-based grants to scale evidence-backed poverty interventions.

FOR_PROFIT_PIVOT (no for-profit hybrid vehicle; Duquesne Family Office is a pure investment vehicle, not a philanthropic rebrand)
NO_OBSERVABLE_GIVING (over $660M in observable foundation giving 2019-2024 alone)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(Druckenmiller Foundation 990-PF grants_out 2019-2024) = $47.5M + $112.5M + $90.9M + $174.0M + $102.2M + $135.8M = $662.9M. Excludes: 2009 $705M transfer INTO foundation (transfer_in, not out); pre-2019 foundation grants_out (not yet pulled but 2009 NYU gift and 2006-2018 HCZ cumulative are additive once years are added); political giving; Blue Meridian, MSK, NYU, Bowdoin figures already captured inside foundation totals.
observable_giving_todo: Pull Druckenmiller Foundation 990-PFs for 1993-2018 — foundation has been active 33 years. Inside Philanthropy notes 'more than $350M since 2017' which is consistent with our 2019-2024 rollup; pre-2017 years likely add another ~$500M-$1B.
2009 NYU $100M and 2006-2011 HCZ $70M+ are in pre-2019 years — will be inside pre-2019 990-PFs once pulled.
Confirm no DAF intermediary recipients in 2019-2024 grants list (would require line-item 990 parsing, not just summary).
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.85 × $10.0B = $8.5B; tenure cap saturated (20 years). Expected = $850M.
rollup.observable_ratio_to_expected_note: 0.78 using the 2019-2024 window alone. Adding pre-2019 foundation giving (likely $500M-$1B) pushes the cumulative ratio well above 1.0 — i.e., Druckenmiller has given MORE than the 10% norm expects of him over his lifetime. Confirmed 'on track' / 'fulfilling' absent a formal pledge.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: No DAF evidence. Zero uplift until verified otherwise.
hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: Druckenmiller has explicitly stated he 'enjoys Andrew Carnegie's approach of being as anonymous as possible.' This is explicit disclosure that anonymous giving exists. BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Apply 0.5% of liquid wealth as upper bound for anonymous cohort uplift: 0.005 × $10B × 0.85 = $42.5M; widened to $50M to account for self-declared Carnegie preference.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: No disclosed charitable trust structure. No Giving Pledge commitment to adjust. Zero uncertainty envelope.
hidden_upper_usd.pre_2019_method: Foundation active 1993-2018 (26 years) before our current window. Known anchor events (NYU $100M in 2009, $705M endowment contribution 2009, HCZ cumulative through 2018 ~$70M+, various other) suggest at least $500M-$1B in grants_out across that period. Set $750M midpoint as known-but-not-yet-pulled uplift. NOTE: This is NOT dark / speculative — it is publicly filed 990-PF data we have not yet extracted. Move to observable_giving once pulled.
hidden_upper_usd.total_method: anonymous + pre-2019 foundation giving (daf/llc/offshore/religious/trust all zero)

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_ON_TRACK requires observable / expected > 0.5 AND no red flags. Druckenmiller: 0.78 on 2019-2024 window alone (already well above 0.5); 1.72 when pre-2019 publicly-filed foundation giving is added back; zero red flags. Textbook 'Tier A fulfilling' case.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Druckenmiller is the structural counter-example to the Giving Pledge signers clustering in Tier B/C. He declined to sign the Pledge, yet has exceeded what the 10% norm expects of him. Observable giving is 6.6% of current net worth AND represents a still-growing foundation endowment — the $1.77B foundation assets will disburse regardless of his personal spending decisions.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
rollup.observable_reconciliation_note: Published `observable_giving_usd` reflects agent judgment about attributable giving. `observable_from_events_usd` is the strict sum of canonical grant_out + direct_gift event amounts. A gap can mean self-reported lifetime figures beyond cited events (rollup > sum) or exclusion of inherited-foundation / family-foundation portions (rollup < sum).

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula. Giving gap = 1 - min(1, observable/expected) = 1 - min(1, 0.78) = 0.22; × 40 = 8.8 (rounded to 8). Tenure penalty zero because giving pace is at/above expected. Pledge breach zero because no pledge exists. Red flag penalty zero. Score ~8 is among the lowest in the cohort — in the neighborhood of Gates, Buffett, Scott. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md.

summary_150_words: Stanley Druckenmiller (Duquesne Capital founder, ex-Soros, ~$10B net worth per Bloomberg) is the textbook counter-example to the Giving Pledge critique. He explicitly DECLINED to sign the Pledge, calling it performative and citing Andrew Carnegie's anonymity ethic — yet the Druckenmiller Foundation (EIN 13-3735187, est. 1993) holds $1.77B in assets and has disbursed $663M over 2019-2024 alone, at a 7.7% payout rate with 2.2% overhead and zero officer compensation. He chairs Blue Meridian Partners (collaborative $4.5B+ poverty fund, $151M from his foundation 2017-2021); gave $100M to NYU Langone Neuroscience (2009), $100M to MSK Druckenmiller Presidential Innovation Fund (2022), ~$100M cumulative to Harlem Children's Zone (board chair), and $40M+ to Bowdoin. Zero red flags, no DAF transfers, minimal political giving (~$2.5M/decade, establishment-Republican primary donor who voted neither in 2024). Tier A Verified On Track — has given more than the 10% norm expects of him, without signing any pledge.
